basic_details.job_title: CSIR CFTRI Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for Project Associate I Posts
basic_details.organization_name: CSIR-Central Food Technological Research Institute, Mysuru
basic_details.short_information: CSIR-Central Food Technological Research Institute, Mysuru (CSIR-CFTRI) has released the official Project Associate I Notification 2026 on its official website. A total of 2 vacancies have been announced under the CSIR-CFTRI Project Associate-I Recruitment 2026. Eligible candidates up to 35 years of age with an M.Sc. in Food Science and Technology, Nutrition, Biochemistry, or Biotechnology can apply online up to 07 July 2026. The official notification PDF and online application link are available below.

age_limit.details: Maximum Age Limit: 35 years Age Relaxation: SC/ST/PwBD/Women: 5 years, OBC: 3 years

qualification.education_qualification: M.Sc. in Food Science and Technology or Nutrition or Biochemistry or Biotechnology
qualification.details: Project Associate I (GAP678-01): M.Sc. in Food Science and Technology or Nutrition from a recognised university or equivalent. Project Associate I (GAP678-02): M.Sc. in Biochemistry or Biotechnology from a recognized University or equivalent.

how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online on or before 07-07-2026.
To apply directly, use the Apply Online link provided in the Important Links section.
Alternatively, candidates may visit the official website of CSIR-CFTRI to complete the application.
Fill out the application form carefully with correct personal, educational, and contact details.
Upload required documents such as photograph, signature, and certificates in the prescribed format.
Pay the application fee online, if applicable.
Review all details carefully before submitting the form.
Take a printout or save a copy of the submitted application for future reference.

rows.csir_central_food_technological_research: M.Sc. in Food Science and Technology or Nutrition or Biochemistry or Biotechnology

content_sections.heading: CSIR-CFTRI Vacancy 2026 Notification PDF
content_sections.text: Candidates who are interested in applying for the CSIR-CFTRI Recruitment 2026 should first download and carefully read the official notification PDF. The notification contains complete details about vacancy distribution, eligibility criteria, age limit, stipend/salary, selection process, important dates, and application instructions. Reading the official document is important to ensure that you meet all requirements before submitting the application form. Download CSIR-CFTRI Job 2026 Notification PDF

content_sections.heading: CSIR-CFTRI Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Project Associate I (GAP678-01): M.Sc. in Food Science and Technology or Nutrition from a recognised university or equivalent. Project Associate I (GAP678-02): M.Sc. in Biochemistry or Biotechnology from a recognized University or equivalent.

content_sections.heading: CSIR-CFTRI Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit
content_sections.text: Maximum Age Limit: 35 years Age Relaxation: SC/ST/PwBD/Women: 5 years, OBC: 3 years

content_sections.heading: How to Apply
content_sections.text: Applications must be submitted online on or before 07-07-2026. To apply directly, use the Apply Online link provided in the Important Links section. Alternatively, candidates may visit the official website of CSIR-CFTRI to complete the application. Fill out the application form carefully with correct personal, educational, and contact details. Upload required documents such as photograph, signature, and certificates in the prescribed format. Pay the application fee online, if applicable. Review all details carefully before submitting the form. Take a printout or save a copy of the submitted application for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the CSIR-CFTRI Project Associate - I recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the application form for the CSIR-CFTRI Project Associate - I positions is 07 July 2026. Ensure your application is submitted before this deadline.

faq.question: Q2. What are the educational qualifications required for the Project Associate - I posts?
faq.answer: For one post, an M.Sc. in Food Science and Technology or Nutrition is required. For the other post, an M.Sc. in Biochemistry or Biotechnology is necessary. Both must be from a recognized university.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for the Project Associate - I positions?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for all Project Associate - I posts is 35 years. Age relaxation is applicable for SC/ST/PwBD/Women and OBC categories as per government rules.

faq.question: Q4. Is there any application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee mentioned for any category (General/OBC, SC/ST) for this recruitment drive.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary offered for the Project Associate - I posts?
faq.answer: The selected candidates will receive a monthly salary ranging from Rs. 25,000 to Rs. 31,000, potentially with HRA, depending on the specific project and qualification.

faq.question: Q6. How many vacancies are available for the Project Associate - I position?
faq.answer: There are a total of 2 vacancies for the Project Associate - I posts at CSIR-CFTRI, Mysuru.

faq.question: Q7. What is the mode of application for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The application process for the CSIR-CFTRI Project Associate - I recruitment is entirely online through the official CSIR-CFTRI website.

faq.question: Q8. What is the selection process for these positions?
faq.answer: The selection process for the Project Associate - I posts will be based on an interview. Candidates shortlisted based on their applications will be called for the interview.

faq.question: Q9. Is this a permanent job?
faq.answer: No, the Project Associate - I positions are contractual in nature. The duration of the contract will be specified in the appointment letter.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I find the official notification and apply online?
faq.answer: You can find the official notification and the online application link in the "Important Links" section of this article. It is recommended to download and read the notification before applying.
